classification.gcvs.reason: Variable-star and spectral classifications remain source object/system scoped until public-name identity and component applicability are resolved without copying system evidence onto a star.

classification.vsx.reason: VSX OID, public-name, Gaia-name, variability, and classification scopes require an explicit identity/applicability policy before selection.

clusters.hunt_reffert_2024.reason: Cluster physical solutions and probability-bearing memberships need separate authority, probability, and non-containment selection policies.

compact.atnf.reason: Pulsar timing, spin, position, association, and classification contexts require compact-object applicability and coherent-context selection.

compact.mcgill_magnetar.reason: Distinct timing, X-ray, distance, association, and activity contexts require compact-object quantity-specific applicability policies.

exoplanet_lifecycle.exoplanet_eu.reason: Supplemental lifecycle assertions must be reconciled with NASA and M5.3 while retaining conflicts, transitions, negative evidence, and tombstones.

exoplanet_lifecycle.hwc.reason: Habitability-context lifecycle evidence needs identity reconciliation and must not create canonical planet inventory or status independently.

exoplanet_lifecycle.open_exoplanet_catal.reason: OEC lifecycle and parameter evidence requires conflict-preserving reconciliation against the canonical NASA-led planet projection.

extended.green_snr.reason: Supernova-remnant physical evidence requires extended-object identity and public-projection policy; it must not enter stellar facts.

extended.openngc_and_nebulae.reason: Extended-object identities, aliases, constituent markers, geometry, and distances require a separate extended-object projection policy.

gaia.dr3.astrophysical_parameters_supp.reason: Supplementary AP activity, extinction, chemistry, and evolutionary quantities need domain-specific authority and coherent-set policies.

gaia.dr3.external_crossmatches.reason: Official external-crossmatch rows are identity/scope evidence and are not independently selectable scientific quantities.

gaia.dr3.non_single_star.reason: NSS fitted solutions lack inspectable component endpoints and need reviewed relation binding before orbit projection.

identity.simbad.reason: SIMBAD identifiers, object types, classifications, and bibliography need separate alias, identity, and scientific-applicability decisions.

multiplicity.debcat.reason: Primary and secondary component physics requires accepted endpoint bindings; integrated metallicity and photometry must remain system scoped.

multiplicity.el_badry_2021_wide_binary.reason: Probability-bearing wide-pair claims require independent endpoint binding and relation selection without manufacturing containment.

multiplicity.msc.reason: MSC component classifications, physics, hierarchy, and orbits require reusable component-label and endpoint reconciliation.

multiplicity.orb6.reason: Combined pair designations remain identity evidence until both orbit endpoints and pair scope resolve uniquely.

multiplicity.sb9.reason: Primary/secondary classifications and orbital solutions require accepted component endpoint bindings rather than system-name matching.

multiplicity.sbx.reason: Binary and hierarchy endpoint scopes must resolve independently before component classifications or orbits become selected facts.

multiplicity.wds.reason: WDS summary classifications describe visual pairs and cannot be copied onto individual stars without reviewed component scope.

multiplicity.wds_gaia_xmatch.reason: WDS-to-Gaia rows are component endpoint crossmatch evidence, not standalone scientific quantities.

naming.iau_wgsn.reason: Official proper names and Bayer component ambiguity require an explicit naming authority and object-scope selection policy.

solar_system.jpl_horizons_artificial.reason: Artificial-spacecraft controls verify source and orbit contracts but are excluded from the public natural-object selection projection.

solar_system.jpl_horizons_authority.reason: Natural Solar System state vectors and orbital elements require epoch/frame-aware authority and projection policies.

tess.identity_and_candidate_evidence.reason: TOI candidates, confirmations, false positives, and transitions must preserve lifecycle polarity and remain separate from canonical planet counts.

tess.villanova_eb.reason: TESS eclipsing-binary target, sector, status, and flag evidence needs host/component binding and relation-aware projection.

ultracool.gaia_dr3_sample.reason: This source contains probability-bearing ultracool-sample membership context but no independent stellar parameter or classification measurement; membership remains inspectable evidence and cannot invent a selected spectral class.
